articles.title: UAE condemns Iranian attacks in Gulf states
articles.summary: UAE condemns Iranian attacks in Gulf states The Foreign Ministry of the United Arab Emirates issued a statement condemning Iranian attacks in Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan.  "The United Arab Emirates has condemned in the strongest terms the renewed hostile attacks by Iran targeting the Kingdom of Bahrain, the State of Kuwait, and the Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan with drones and missiles," the statement said. The statement reiterated the UAE's "full solidarity" with the targeted Gulf states and "support for all measures aimed at safeguarding their security".

articles.title: Netanyahu cancels trip to the US scheduled for next week
articles.summary: Netanyahu cancels trip to the US scheduled for next week Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has cancelled a trip to the US which was scheduled for next week. He will not travel due to the postponement of the funeral of Senator Lindsey Graham, his office said. A day prior to the cancellation, a senior Israeli official said that Netanyahu wanted to meet Trump during the visit, but it was not yet clear if he would be able to, according to Reuters. A White House official denied there were any meetings scheduled between Trump and Netanyahu, Axios reported.
